Patch Set 5:

I created the patch based on the sanity-hsm.sh and test-framework.sh in http://review.whamcloud.com/6913 . In order to make autotest run sanity-hsm.sh with copytool installed, I made this patch depend on a temporary patch http://review.whamcloud.com/7107 which contains the latest version of the following patches:

 LU-1333 hsm: Add hsm_release feature. http://review.whamcloud.com/7028
 LU-3343 mdt: HSM coordinator main thread http://review.whamcloud.com/6912
 LU-3363 api: HSM import uses new released pattern http://review.whamcloud.com/6536
 LU-2062 utils: HSM Posix CopyTool http://review.whamcloud.com/4737
 LU-3561 tests: HSM sanity test suite http://review.whamcloud.com/6913

We'll see the test result. Please review the patch. Thanks.

Patch Set 5: (13 inline comments)

The initial idea was to have the agent seen as device (like OST) for which the device name was the backend path ($ARC).
This device should also mount the Lustre client at start and we should be able to start/stop the CT on it without unmounting the device

Patch Set 8:

This patch set mainly improves test 52, 53 and 106 to avoid unmounting $MOUNT2 so that we can specify $MOUNT2 to $SHARED_DIRECTORY in case there is no shared directory among client/agent nodes in the test environment.

With this patch, sanity-hsm.sh passed with remote agent and SHARED_DIRECTORY="/mnt/lustre2":

https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/288f0f6a-fe78-11e2-afbf-52540035b04c

Excepted tests are: 12 13 24 30 31 52 58 59 110 201 222 223 225 226, which failed with local agent.

Patch Set 9:

It occurred to me that in sanity-hsm.sh, it tends to start/stop CT for individual tests. This is not good because it won't reveal the problem of CT itself. So I tend to think we should start it once at the beginning of sanity-hsm and kill it after all test cases finish.
Patch Set 9: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Uploaded patch set 10.

Patch Set 10:

Initialize variable "n" with "0" to fix the following failure in patch set 9:

 CMD: fat-intel-2vm7 /usr/sbin/lctl get_param -n mdt.lustre-MDT0000.hsm.agent_actions | awk '/'0x200000401:0x8:0x0'.*action='RESTORE'/ {n++}; END {print n}'
  sanity-hsm test_12d: @@@@@@ FAIL: restore non archived must not make a request
Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 10: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/75308664-0cb5-11e3-a66c-52540035b04c).  Ran 14 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-hsm.

Patch Set 10: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

"It occurred to me that in sanity-hsm.sh, it tends to start/stop CT for individual tests. This is not good because it won't reveal the problem of CT itself. "
We did this to avoid side effect between tests which are very difficult to track. sanity-hsm tests are made to test basic features of HSM. Load test with the same CT have to be done in new tests (like the 40).
Another solution is to have sanity-hsm run in 2 modes :
- as today with 1 CT per test
- with the same CT for all tests
This mean adding a new acc_sm test

Patch Set 10:

Hi JC,

I didn't modify the patch, just an idea. The problem to start copytool for each test is that we won't have any chance to test copytool itself.

Jinshan

Patch Set 10:

If the copytool is not restarted between each test, this could lead to lot of side effects.

The copytool is already tested as it manages each transfer for each tests. Some tests like #40 archives a lot of files. 
If you want to exercise the copytool for a long period doing a mix of archive/restore, this is no more unit test, but functional test, not sure sanity-hsm is the good place for that. Or, we could add specific tests...

Patch Set 10:

What about leaving the changes to be made in another patch and make this patch only implement remote agent support?

I've no idea why sanity-hsm test failed in patch set 10 because Maloo is down and I cannot check the test results. By applying patch set 10 on the latest master branch, I just manually ran sanity-hsm.sh on 2 clients + 1 MGS/MDS + 1 OSS (7 OSTs), the test passed.

After Maloo is up, I'll check the failure and see whether the patch needs to be updated or not.

Patch Set 10:

Aurelien, I see. Thanks for explanation.

Patch Set 10:

Toro has started the test process

Patch Set 10:

> By applying patch set 10 on the latest master branch, I just manually ran sanity-hsm.sh on 2 clients + 1 MGS/MDS + 1 OSS (7 OSTs), the test passed.

Here is the test report: https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/68457cbc-0f9d-11e3-81e3-52540035b04c

Patch Set 10: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/bea71050-0fe0-11e3-81e3-52540035b04c).  Ran 16 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 10:

Hi JC,

What about leaving additional sanity-hsm.sh changes/improvements to be made in other patches and make this patch only implement remote agent support? Currently, the sanity-hsm.sh passed in both manual and autotest runs.

Patch Set 10: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Yes my -1 was because of the addition changes targeted. With just the remote agent support it is perfect.

Patch Set 10:

Thank you, JC!

commit.subject: LU-3561 tests: improve sanity-hsm.sh to support remote agent
commit.message:
LU-3561 tests: improve sanity-hsm.sh to support remote agent

In the current sanity-hsm.sh, only one HSM copytool is started on
the local Lustre client node.

In order to test multiple remote HSM agents/copytools, this patch
introduces a new facet type named "AGT" and the following associated
variables:

- AGTCOUNT: number of agents
- AGTDEV{N}: target HSM mount point (root path of the backend)
- agt{N}_HOST: hostname of the agent agt{N}
- SINGLEAGT: facet of the single agent

By default, only single copytool is started on a remote client node.
If there was no remote client, then the copytool will be started on
the local client.

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I6e85d6948da894bd50148b0504347159ae23a79b
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7110
Tested-by: Hudson
Reviewed-by: jacques-Charles Lafoucriere <jacques-charles.lafoucriere@cea.fr>
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
